items.therapeutic_goal: Support safer separations, reduce separation worries, and build practical brave separation steps.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child has distress around separation from caregivers at school, bedtime, or daily transitions.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for separation distress, safe goodbye planning, school drop-off fear, and sleeping-alone concerns.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Reduce fear avoidance in specific phobias through graded brave steps, thought support, and coping plans.
items.when_to_use: Use for specific fear targets such as dogs, elevators, shots, loud noises, and similar phobia patterns.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for specific phobia fears and practical fear-ladder coping support.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Support social courage, reduce social fear thoughts, and build practical social participation skills.
items.when_to_use: Use for fear of speaking, joining peers, asking for help, or embarrassment in social/school settings.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for social anxiety, class-speaking fear, and social confidence building.
items.safety_notes: Filename mismatch resolved by worksheet content: this PDF is clinically Social Anxiety (1.3), not Specific Phobias.

items.title_en: Children CBT Specialized 1.4 — Test & Performance Anxiety
items.therapeutic_goal: Reduce test/performance anxiety by preparing routines, thought support, and step-based task planning.
items.when_to_use: Use for freezing before tests, fear of mistakes, and high stress in performance situations.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for test fear, performance pressure, and pre-task calming routines.
items.safety_notes: Filename mismatch resolved by worksheet content: this PDF is clinically Test & Performance Anxiety (1.4), not Specific Phobias.

items.title_en: Children CBT Specialized 1.5 — Generalized Anxiety / Everyday Worries
items.therapeutic_goal: Reduce broad worry load by clarifying controllables, practicing calming anchors, and structured worry coping.
items.when_to_use: Use for frequent everyday worries about family, school, future, and uncertain daily situations.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for generalized worry patterns and everyday anxiety support.
items.safety_notes: Filename mismatch resolved by worksheet content: this PDF is clinically Generalized Anxiety / Everyday Worries (1.5), not Specific Phobias.

items.title_en: Children CBT Specialized 2.1 — Anger Outbursts & Regulation
items.therapeutic_goal: Improve anger regulation through early-signal awareness, pause skills, and repair planning.
items.when_to_use: Use for frustration outbursts, escalation patterns, and regulation coaching after anger spikes.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for child anger outbursts and regulation planning.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.title_en: Children CBT Specialized 2.2 — Oppositional Behavior / ODD Patterns
items.therapeutic_goal: Support cooperation, reduce argument cycles, and build flexible response patterns in authority conflicts.
items.when_to_use: Use for frequent arguing, refusal, and conflict around instructions or authority requests.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for oppositional behavior patterns with cooperation-focused CBT support.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Strengthen stop-think-choose behavior to reduce impulsive reactions and improve repair skills.
items.when_to_use: Use for acting before thinking, interrupting, impulsive behavior, and turn-waiting difficulties.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for impulsive actions and stop-think-choose coaching.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Strengthen self-worth and kinder self-talk while reducing harsh self-criticism patterns.
items.when_to_use: Use when a child says negative things about self-worth, competence, or belonging.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for low self-esteem, self-criticism, and confidence-building support.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Improve social participation, communication, repair skills, and friendship support behaviors.
items.when_to_use: Use for peer-entry difficulties, conflict with peers, social cue confusion, or social exclusion stress.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for social skills coaching and friendship problem-solving.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Support response flexibility and urge tolerance in OCD-like loops while reducing ritual dependence.
items.when_to_use: Use for sticky thoughts, ritual urges, repeated checking/washing urges, and reassurance-loop patterns.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for pediatric OCD patterns and brave new-response practice.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.title_en: Children CBT Specialized 4.2 — Trauma-Sensitive Coping / PTSD
items.therapeutic_goal: Support stabilization, safety, grounding, and gentle regulation after fear/stress activation.
items.when_to_use: Use only for present-moment safety and grounding support after fear responses; avoid trauma-detail processing.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for trauma-sensitive stabilization, grounding, and safety support without trauma processing.
items.safety_notes: Trauma-sensitive coping only: focus on safety, grounding, body regulation, and support. Do not elicit trauma details and do not suggest exposure processing.

items.therapeutic_goal: Improve bedtime regulation, reduce night worries, and build consistent sleep-support routines.
items.when_to_use: Use for bedtime anxiety, fear of dark, wake-night distress, and sleep-routine support needs.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for child sleep worries and bedtime coping plans.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.therapeutic_goal: Support stress-linked body complaints with regulation, support-seeking, and return-to-action planning.
items.when_to_use: Use for stress-linked stomach aches, headaches, and body discomfort patterns around school or worries.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for psychosomatic/stress-body complaints in children with coping-focused support.
items.safety_notes: Children-focused CBT supportive worksheets. Keep guidance gentle, non-shaming, and age-appropriate.

items.title_en: Children CBT Specialized 5.3 — Enuresis / Encopresis Stress Support
items.therapeutic_goal: Provide shame-free emotional support around stress-linked toileting accidents while strengthening coping and support plans.
items.when_to_use: Use for stress-supportive emotional coping around enuresis/encopresis experiences, not as medical treatment.
items.ai_matching_summary: Match for shame-reduction and stress-support planning in enuresis/encopresis contexts.
items.safety_notes: Emotional/stress support only for enuresis/encopresis context: no shame, no blame, never imply intentional behavior, and avoid presenting as medical treatment. Persistent physical symptoms should be discussed with a qualified clinician/doctor.
